FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class DragController

Namespace
FluentGpu.Input
Assembly
FluentGpu.Input.dll

The drag-reorder gesture engine (E5). A left press on (or inside) a CanDrag node ARMS a candidate; pointer travel outside the drag box PROMOTES it from a click to a drag (the dispatcher then suppresses the click and routes every move here — capture semantics). The promoted drag draws its visual on the dragged node itself: a parent-space LocalTransform translate + 0.8 opacity + a flyout-class shadow (WinUI ListViewItemDragThemeOpacity = 0.80 — microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\ListViewItem_themeresources.xaml:7), and the node stops hit-testing so drop-target queries see THROUGH the moving visual.

Threshold: the Windows drag box is SM_CXDRAG/SM_CYDRAG (4px default), tested per-axis (dx > maxDx || dy > maxDy — microsoft-ui-xaml dxaml\xcp\dxaml\lib\ListViewBaseItem_Partial.cpp:1864-1878). WinUI's list items double it (LISTVIEWBASEITEM_MOUSE_DRAG_THRESHOLD_MULTIPLIER = 2.0, same file :54); this engine uses the 4px base box per plan E5, matching ClickSlopPx.

Axis-aware arena-lite (promotion-time arbitration): the item's reorder axis is its PARENT container's main axis (row → horizontal item-drag). A gesture whose dominant axis is PERPENDICULAR to that, with a scrollable ancestor that actually overflows along the gesture axis, yields to the pan (vertical pan over a scrollable beats a horizontal item-drag) — the WinUI manipulation-arena outcome for a tab strip inside a scrolling page.

Drop-settle rides the existing FLIP pipeline: Complete restores the resting visuals and reports (draggedRect → restingRect) through OnSettle; the host wires that to AnimEngine.AnimateBounds, so the seeded position spring is retargeted velocity-continuously by the same commit's ApplyProjections when the app's OnDragCompleted handler reorders the collection — displaced siblings and the dropped item all animate through the one layout-transition path (reorder hints come free).

Mid-drag live reorder rides the same pipeline: when the consumer re-projects the children ORDER at the dwell-committed target (ReorderList.ProjectOrder), displaced siblings FLIP to their new slots while Move(Point2, KeyModifiers, uint) RE-ANCHORS the pointer-held visual — it strips the translate it last applied to find the node's CURRENT resting origin, then aims at (grab origin + gesture delta), so a slot move under the pointer (or an ancestor scrolling) never jumps the visual (the WinUI outcome: the dragged item tracks the pointer while MoveItemsForLiveReorder shifts the rest — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:2254). The drag visuals are re-asserted on every move because a mid-drag commit's patch restores the authored opacity/shadow/hit-test (Reconciler ApplyBox writes them unconditionally).

GHOST (E5-L2): while a drag is active the lifted node carries DragGhost and is published as DragGhost — the recorder excludes it from the clipped main pass and re-walks its subtree in an UNCLIPPED top band emitted last (mirroring the orphan pass), so the visual escapes every ancestor scissor (a row dragged out of a clipped list keeps drawing) and paints above overlays — the Flutter/rbd ghost layer.

Spring-lag follow (the rbd feel): the PRESENTED translate eases toward the gesture target with a critically-damped spring (FollowOmega) instead of pinning rigidly — 0-alloc per move/tick. It engages only when the platform delivers real timestamps (consecutive non-zero TimestampMs); 0-stamp gestures (the headless default) snap presented == target, keeping every position assertion deterministic. SpringFollow false disables it outright. The host advances in-between-move easing (and the pointer-pin while an edge auto-scroll moves the resting origin under a still pointer) via Tick(float).

public sealed class DragController

Constructors

DragController(SceneStore, Action) 

public DragController(SceneStore scene, Action requestRerender)

Parameters

scene SceneStore
requestRerender Action

Fields

DragOpacity

Dragged-visual opacity — WinUI ListViewItemDragThemeOpacity = 0.80 (ListViewItem_themeresources.xaml:7, identical in all ThemeDictionaries incl. _perf2026).

public const float DragOpacity = 0.8

Field Value

float

DragShadow

Flyout-class soft shadow under the drag visual (the engine's analytic equivalent of the 32px-depth ThemeShadow WinUI gives lifted drag visuals; values match Dsl.Elevation.Flyout — Input cannot reference Dsl).

public static readonly ShadowSpec DragShadow

Field Value

ShadowSpec

DragThresholdPx

The drag box half-extent, tested per-axis (Win32 SM_CXDRAG/SM_CYDRAG default = 4; ListViewBaseItem_Partial.cpp:1871-1877. WinUI list items apply a ×2 multiplier — see class remarks).

public const float DragThresholdPx = 4

Field Value

float

FollowOmega

Critically-damped spring rate (rad/s) of the ghost's pointer-follow lag — ~150ms visual settle, the react-beautiful-dnd "the ghost breathes behind the pointer" feel. Engine value (the WinUI OLE drag visual is an OS layer with no published spring; the adopted model is Flutter/rbd per the E5 user ruling).

public const float FollowOmega = 38

Field Value

float

OnSettle

Set by the host: the drop-settle seam. Fired after Complete(Point2, KeyModifiers, uint, bool)/Cancel() restored the resting visuals, with the dragged presented rect and the resting rect — wire to AnimEngine.AnimateBounds(node, fromAbs, toAbs, spec) so the visual glides from the drop point into its slot (and is velocity-continuously retargeted by the reorder commit's FLIP pass). Null ⇒ the visual snaps home.

public Action<NodeHandle, RectF, RectF>? OnSettle

Field Value

Action<NodeHandle, RectF, RectF>

Properties

ActiveNode

The node whose drag is in flight (Null when idle/armed). The host's FLIP pass must SKIP this node — the pointer owns its presented transform until the drag ends.

public NodeHandle ActiveNode { get; }

Property Value

NodeHandle

HasActiveWork

True while the presented (spring-lagged) translate is still easing toward the gesture target — the host keeps frames coming and calls Tick(float). Always false for snap-tracking gestures.

public bool HasActiveWork { get; }

Property Value

bool

IsActive

A drag is in flight (threshold crossed; the pointer is owned until release / Escape / cancel).

public bool IsActive { get; }

Property Value

bool

IsArmed

A candidate is armed (press seen, threshold not yet crossed).

public bool IsArmed { get; }

Property Value

bool

SpringFollow

Enable the critically-damped pointer-follow lag (default true). It only engages on gestures whose events carry real platform timestamps — 0-stamp (headless) gestures always track exactly.

public bool SpringFollow { get; set; }

Property Value

bool

VelocityX

The smoothed pointer velocity (px/s, ~50ms EMA) — fed into the L2 DragDropContext session.

public float VelocityX { get; }

Property Value

float

VelocityY 

public float VelocityY { get; }

Property Value

float

Methods

Cancel()

Abort the gesture (Escape / pointer-capture loss / window blur): restore the resting visuals, fire OnDragCanceled, and glide the visual home via OnSettle. A no-op when idle; an armed-only candidate silently disarms (WinUI: a canceled drag never raises a click or a drop).

public void Cancel()

Complete(Point2, KeyModifiers, uint, bool)

Release after an active drag: restore the resting visuals, fire OnDragCompleted (the app commits the reorder here), then hand the (dragged → resting) rects to OnSettle for the FLIP glide. Returns true iff a drag was active — the dispatcher suppresses the click. An armed-only candidate just disarms. suppressSettle (E5-L2): an accepted DROP on a non-reorder target skips the glide — the payload was deposited there, so the visual snaps home instead of springing back (SettleOnDrop opts a reorder target back into the glide).

public bool Complete(Point2 abs, KeyModifiers mods, uint timestampMs, bool suppressSettle = false)

Parameters

abs Point2
mods KeyModifiers
timestampMs uint
suppressSettle bool

Returns

bool

Disarm()

Drop an armed candidate that never promoted (release inside the drag box ⇒ a plain click).

public void Disarm()

Move(Point2, KeyModifiers, uint)

Pointer move while armed or active. Armed: crossing the drag box either PROMOTES (fires OnDragStarted then the first OnDragDelta) or YIELDS to a cross-axis pan (arena-lite) and disarms. Active: applies the parent-space translate and fires OnDragDelta. Returns true iff the gesture owns the pointer after this call (the dispatcher then skips hover/scroll/slider routing).

public bool Move(Point2 abs, KeyModifiers mods, uint timestampMs)

Parameters

abs Point2
mods KeyModifiers
timestampMs uint

Returns

bool

PruneDead()

Called at dispatch start: an armed/active node freed by a reconcile is abandoned WITHOUT touching its (dead) columns — the visual state died with the slot.

public void PruneDead()

Tick(float)

Phase-7 host tick: re-aim at the node's CURRENT resting origin (an edge auto-scroll moves it under a still pointer — the ghost must stay pinned to the grab point) and advance the spring-lag easing between pointer moves. Returns true while the presented translate moved (the host requests the next frame). 0-alloc.

public bool Tick(float dtMs)

Parameters

dtMs float

Returns

bool

TryArm(NodeHandle, Point2, PointerKind, KeyModifiers, uint)

Arm a drag candidate from a left press: walk up from pressTarget for the nearest enabled node carrying DragBit (a press on a child of a draggable row arms the row, like WinUI's item container). Returns false when nothing in the chain is draggable.

public bool TryArm(NodeHandle pressTarget, Point2 abs, PointerKind kind, KeyModifiers mods, uint timestampMs)

Parameters

pressTarget NodeHandle
abs Point2
kind PointerKind
mods KeyModifiers
timestampMs uint

Returns

bool
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
